title: Goniopteris leonina

The classification of Goniopteris leonina is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, the division Pteridophyta, and the class Polypodiopsida. intro: Goniopteris leonina, also known as Leon's cloak fern, is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Goniopteris leonina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
